item.title: Washed in Black (Pearl Jam) w/ Jar of Flies (Alice in Chains) | Annual 90's Tribute Show w/ Two of Seattle's Best!
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

Time for the annual Tribute to the 90s s Best!$20 Advance | $28 DOS | 21+~Washed In Black - A Tribute to Pearl Jam~Jar of Flies - The Ultimate AIC ExperienceAn extremely rare bill..... our schedules dont often align for full sets & we are all super stoked! A show not to miss! Relive some memories, join with friends, & make new ones of both. Come on out to the historic Tractor Tavern and help us Make America Grunge Again - Salud!***THE DEFINITIVE PEARL JAM TRIBUTE***Washed In Black - A Tribute to Pearl JamCome out and join Washed in Black as they take you back to the day when flannel was king From the live vibe of the music, to the energetic performance...... The only thing better are the boys in Pearl Jam themselves! JAR OF FLIES (the ultimate AIC experience) is a collection of top Seattle musicians with a passion for honoring Alice in Chains. This band brings an authentic and powerful sonic experience! This event is 21 and over. Any ticket holder unable to present valid identification indicating that they are at least 21 years of age will not be admitted to this event, and will not be eligible for a refund. item.title: Waku Waku: Anime Rave
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 10 PM

Location: The Crocodile, 2505 1st Ave, Seattle, WA

Dive head first into a high-energy, anime-inspired Rave as WAKU WAKU makes its Seattle debut on Saturday, May 30th, 2026DJ Jak Syn will be throwing down a steady supply of Anime-adjacent jams. Expect to find yourself dancing to a mix of kawaii bass, nightcore, synthwave, future funk, chiptune, EDM remixes & more.Cosplay Highly Encouraged!!!Cosplayers, anime fans, and nightlifers alike, join us in a space where expressive outfits, character-inspired looks, and shared fandom take center stage, whether youre serving main-character energy, chaotic sidekick vibes, or something entirely your own.This is a party for fans of anime themes, cosplay, and artists such as Pegboard Nerds, TeddyLoid, Bemax, Moe Shop, Caramella Girls, Kensuke Ushio, S3RL, Needy Girl Overdose, Geoxor, Ghost Data, Steradlye, and many more. item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Saturday, June 6, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

From The Vera Project

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, June 10, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Mary Gauthier w/ special guests: Celebrating the 20th Anniversary of Mercy Now (partially seated)
item.description:
Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

I am excited to announce that this year is the 20th Anniversary of the release Mercy Now, a record that changed my life. We are celebrating with a vinyl re-release and a tour. Im not really one to look back, but reflecting on these ten songs feels more like standing still long enough to take stock, and appreciating the grace that brought them into existence. These shows will be a continuation of a long conversation with kindred souls, like you, who understand the alchemy of song. Kindred souls who know a sad song doesnt make the darkness deeper; it gives it a shape, a name, shines the light on it, and brings forth hope. Yeah, we all could use a little mercy now This event is 21 and over. item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Friday, June 12, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Alela Diane, guests
item.description:
Thursday, June 18, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Alela Diane: More than a decade into one of contemporary folk’s most quietly extraordinary careers, Alela Diane returns on May 22 with Who's Keeping Time? via Fluff & Gravy / Loose Music. The Portland songwriter’s seventh full-length album came as the consequence of intuition, coincidence, and community. On the first of April last year, Michael Hurley, folk legend and indispensable presence in the Portland music scene, died at the age of eighty-three. “I was absolutely gutted,” says Alela, who didn’t just revere Hurley but knew him well. She performed in a tribute show for Hurley, and in that collective mourning, found solace and inspiration. “It was an epiphany to realize how much I missed my community. I felt very clear about what I wanted in that moment—I want to be alive. I want to see live music. I want to play it.” Though she’d taken the solo route on her last handful of releases, Alela was interested in a new way forward. Plucking the day away in the attic of her 1892 Victorian home, she found new songs flickering in the dusty light—and a desire to play them with people. Alela met drummer Danny Austin-Manning at Clay Street Studios one night and he introduced her to co-producer Sam Weber (Madison Cunningham, Anna Tivel). Along with Sebastian Owens on bass, they recorded fifteen songs in less than five days—all live in the very attic where she wrote them. Anna Tivel contributed backing vocals and violin. Peter Lalish, of the band Lucius, added guitar. Fellow Pacific Northwesterners Kati Claborn and Luke Ydstie of Blind Pilot and AC Sapphire provided overdubs of all kinds. Alela’s vision for uninhibited music and revived, creative kinship thrust her into a new season. The result is an 11-track set ranging from lulling to raw to cinematic, with Alela’s mellifluous voice a lively and affecting instrument throughout. The lyrics reveal an artist with the particular kind of strength required to face pain without getting lost—an ability Alela has honed over a lifetime of songwriting, during which her lustrous discography has garnered major critical acclaim from the likes of Pitchfork, NPR Music, The Guardian, and plenty more. UNCUT has characterized her skill as “insanely beautiful, with the strength and delicacy of spider silk” while counting her work among the ‘50 best singer-songwriter albums’ of all time—a canon comprising John Lennon, Joni Mitchell, Joan Baez, and Paul Simon. Consequence has echoed that significance, declaring, “Hers is a timeless sound, that of a wayfaring troubadour, which only seems to come a few times a generation.” With Who’s Keeping Time?, Alela Diane transforms grief into gathering, reaffirming her place as a vital, living force in contemporary folk music—rooted in community, presence, and the enduring power of song. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Mariel Buckley, guests
item.description:
Sunday, June 28, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Mariel Buckley: Unapologetically genuine, wry and soft-hearted, Mariel Buckley has planted her flag firmly as a songwriter who’s in it for the long haul. She aims to find herself squarely on the fringes and exactly where she belongs: singing and writing songs for the underdogs. Raised in the heart of Canada’s rodeo and country music scenes, she was destined to challenge the boundaries around her, both in life and in song. With a fearless spirit that’s consistently broken through both genre and personal barriers, Buckley’s unique blend of angst-driven alt-folk has made her a standout artist, offering a fresh and dynamic perspective on modern Americana music. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

item.title: The Crocodile Presents: Soda Blonde, guests
item.description:
Sunday, September 27, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $22.50 ($30.90 after fees) Day of: $25 ($33.48 after fees) Soda Blonde: Soda Blonde are a four-piece alt-pop band from Dublin, Ireland, formed in 2019 by Faye O’Rourke (vocals), Adam O’Regan (guitar/keys), Donagh Seaver-O’Leary (bass) and Dylan Lynch (drums). The group emerged from the ashes of critically acclaimed indie rock outfit Little Green Cars, moving toward a bolder, more cinematic and emotionally charged sound that blends evocative lyrics with adventurous production. Their music traverses the tension between introspection and ambition, pairing O’Rourke’s distinctive, expressive vocals with genre-defying arrangements that draw comparisons to synth-infused art-pop and emotionally honest songwriting. Soda Blonde’s debut single “Swimming Through the Night” immediately marked a stylistic shift for the members, establishing the band’s commitment to cinematic hooks and atmospheric grooves. The band’s first studio album Small Talk (2021) showcased polished, self-produced songwriting and was nominated for the Choice Music Prize. Their sophomore record Dream Big (2023) expanded their sonic palette and solidified their reputation as one of Ireland’s most compelling contemporary acts. In 2025, Soda Blonde released the People Pleaser EP and completed an ambitious live orchestral project with the National Symphony Orchestra at Dublin’s National Concert Hall, captured in both a concert film and a live album. Known for their emotionally charged live performances, bold visuals and genre-spanning sound, Soda Blonde continue to push beyond conventional pop boundaries while maintaining a deeply personal connection to their lyrics and visual storytelling. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern
